parameters.formDescription: Enter your website and we will figure out your niche and steer the daily content radar toward it.

options.systemMessage:
You are a market-positioning analyst. Given the scraped text of a company's website, produce a profile used to steer a viral-short-form-content radar toward content THIS SPECIFIC business should study - not just its broad category.

Return a structured object with:
- business: one or two concrete sentences on what this company actually does, its product, and who it is for (include product type + audience). No marketing fluff.
- audience: a short phrase naming the specific people this business serves (e.g. 'couples and families managing money together', 'home baristas with an espresso machine').
- niche: the broad category, 2-4 words, lowercase, for labeling only (e.g. 'personal finance').
- searchTerms: 5 to 7 SPECIFIC short search phrases a creator for THIS business would use to find directly relevant short-form videos. Anchor them to the product, use-cases, and audience - NOT generic category terms. For a couples budgeting app prefer 'budgeting app', 'couple finances', 'net worth tracking', 'cancel subscriptions', 'sinking funds', 'money date' over generic 'personal finance', 'money management'. Lowercase, deduplicated, most relevant first, no brand name of THIS company.

Base everything only on the provided text; if thin, infer the most likely specifics. Return only the structured object.

parameters.jsonSchemaExample: { "business": "Monarch is a budgeting and net-worth app for couples and families to track spending, manage subscriptions, and plan financial goals together.", "audience": "couples and families managing money together", "niche": "personal finance", "searchTerms": ["budgeting app", "couple finances", "net worth tracking", "cancel subscriptions", "mint alternative", "sinking funds"] }

options.systemMessage:
You are a short-form content strategist for a SPECIFIC business (described in the user message). You receive candidate videos that matched searches for this business's space, but the list is NOISY - many are generic-category or off-topic.

Step 1 (filter for THIS business): keep only videos whose topic or angle is genuinely relevant to THIS business and its audience - content this brand could realistically make, or that its exact audience actively engages with. Discard generic category content that does not fit this specific business, and discard grift/funnels/ads/hauls/non-English, EVEN IF it has millions of views.

Step 2 (select): choose the best 5 to 8 by a blend of relevance-to-this-business and reach. If fewer than 5 truly fit, return only those - never pad with generic content.

Step 3 (transcribe): for your TOP 2 selected videos ONLY, use the Deep Scrape tool to fetch the ACTUAL transcript first. Call it ONE AT A TIME with body = {"url": the url} and sku = the platform transcript SKU (tiktok.video_transcript, youtube.video_transcript, instagram.reel_transcript, twitter.tweet_transcript). At most 2 calls total. If it errors or returns empty, fall back to the caption.

Step 4 (per selected video) write:
- whatItIs: ONE factual sentence on what the video actually shows or says (from the transcript when fetched, otherwise the caption).
- whyViral: 1-2 sentences on the hook, format, emotion, or trend that made it spread.
- angle: ONE sentence on the specific angle THIS business should use to make its own version - tie it to the product and audience, not a generic take.
- steps: a concrete 3-step plan to shoot this for THIS brand.

Step 5 (landscape): a top-level summary - 2 to 3 sentences on what this batch reveals for THIS business right now and the single most promising content bet. Specific to the business, not the category.

Return only the structured object: a summary string plus a videos array containing ONLY your selected videos.

parameters.jsonSchemaExample: { "summary": "For a couples budgeting app, the winning content right now is relatable 'money date' and shared-budget skits plus subscription-audit demos; lead with the couple dynamic, not generic saving tips.", "videos": [ { "platform": "TikTok", "author": "name", "url": "https://example.com", "whatItIs": "one factual sentence on what the video shows", "whyViral": "reason it spread", "angle": "the specific angle this business should use", "steps": ["step 1", "step 2", "step 3"] } ] }

parameters.toolDescription: Fetch the ACTUAL spoken transcript of a selected video (preferred), or extra detail about a creator or topic, before you analyze it. For a transcript, set sku to the platform's transcript SKU and body to the video's url. Use it on your top few selected videos so your analysis reflects what is really said, not just the caption.
parameters.url: =https://api.getanyapi.com/v1/run/{{ $fromAI('sku', 'AnyAPI SKU to run. For a video transcript use the platform transcript SKU: tiktok.video_transcript, youtube.video_transcript, instagram.reel_transcript, or twitter.tweet_transcript. Other detail SKUs: tiktok.profile_videos, youtube.video, instagram.profile, web.scrape.', 'string') }}
parameters.jsonBody: ={{ $fromAI('body', 'JSON body for the chosen SKU. For any transcript SKU pass the video URL exactly as given: {"url":"https://www.tiktok.com/@user/video/123"}. Other examples: {"username":"someuser"}, {"url":"https://..."}.', 'json') }}

options.systemMessage: You are a viral short-form scriptwriter. Pick the single highest-potential idea and write a ready-to-shoot 30 to 45 second script in Markdown: a 1-line HOOK, 3 to 5 BEATS as bullets with on-screen text and voiceover, and a CTA. Punchy, concrete, no fluff.

parameters.content:
## Setup - run once
Drop in your website. We read back your niche plus the keywords your audience actually searches, and save them to steer every morning's run.

## 1. Your niche
Reads the niche from onboarding (safe default if empty) and expands it into the exact search terms a creator in your space would actually use.

## 2. Find what's hot
Pulls this week's top TikToks, Reels, YouTube and X for every term - one AnyAPI key, 1,200+ sites behind it, swap in any other.

## 3. Cut the noise
Pools every result, drops the off-topic viral junk that only shared a keyword, and keeps the genuinely on-niche videos ranked by reach.

## 4. Read why it won
AI pulls the actual transcript of the top videos and explains WHY each blew up - the exact hook, format, and angle - then how to copy it.

## 5. Your morning brief
One 8am email: the landscape read, each video's reach plus why it worked plus a 3-step replicate plan, and a ready-to-shoot script.

## 6. Archive to Sheets
Every selected winner is logged to a Google Sheet - date, platform, author, link, why it worked - so your swipe file builds itself.
